items.name: stick of censor's wax
items.description: Deep red wax cut with a censor's mark. Seals a document into fact, or a spell into something the Empire would recognise.

items.name: vial of permit ink
items.description: Iron-gall ink keyed to imperial forms. Precious for forgers, and for spells that want the weight of the written word.

items.name: pinch of ledger dust
items.description: Fine graphite swept from under a clerk's desk, thick with the residue of other people's debts.

items.name: broken confiscation seal
items.description: A lead seal snapped off some seized wonder. It remembers being the moment a thing stopped being yours.

items.name: censor's measuring lens
items.spellBias: measurement, scrutiny, reducing wonder to fact
items.description: A brass-rimmed lens censors use to reduce a marvel to a number. Focused, it lends a spell cold, exacting precision.

items.name: warden's lead baton
items.description: A lead-cored baton issued to yard wardens. Heavy with the confidence of a uniform.

items.name: marble-scaled ward plate
items.spellBias: law made solid, cold protection, weight
items.description: A cuirass faced with thin marble scales, cold and heavy and quietly official. It turns blows and the odd accusation alike.

items.name: stick of charter chalk
items.spellBias: charter geometry, drawn law, exact figures
items.description: Dense white chalk that draws charter geometry cleanly on any surface. A focus for anyone learning the Empire's lawful magic.

modifier.focusBias: charter geometry, lawful figures, drawn boundaries

items.name: inspector's waxed gloves
items.description: Waxed grey gloves for handling seized wonders without leaving a mark or catching a curse.

items.name: signed compliance writ
items.description: A writ declaring its bearer compliant and free to pass. Whether it is genuine depends entirely on how it was obtained.

items.name: blank imperial permit
items.description: A pre-stamped permit with the particulars left blank. In the right hands, it becomes whatever it needs to say.

items.name: numbered evidence tag
items.spellBias: provenance, custody, the number of a thing
items.description: A brass tag on a wire loop. Whatever it was tied to is elsewhere now; the number remembers.

items.name: confiscated wonder
items.spellBias: seized magic, forbidden marvel, restless power
items.description: A small marvel the censors seized and mislabelled: it hums when no one is measuring it. Evidence, contraband, focus, or gift, depending on who wants it.

items.name: warden's signal whistle
items.description: A shrill brass whistle that calls other wardens running. Useful to raise the yard, or to make someone think you have.
